title: TC001-Create a new product and verify it appears in the products table
description: Verifies an admin can add a product via the form and see it listed in the products table.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin/login (explicit test step).
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'admin' into the username field (index 1297), then type 'password123' into the password field (index 1301), then click the Sign in button (index 1304).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click on 'Products' in the admin navigation (click element index 1505).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/aside/nav/a[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Add Product' button (index 1626) to open the Add Product form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/main/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the Add Product form (name, code, price, select category, description) and click the Add Product submit button (index 2169) to create the product.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Automated Test Product')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('54321')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('199')
        
        # -> Select a category for the product, enter a short description, and submit the Add Product form so the product is created (then the page will be checked for the new product listing).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[6]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Automated product created by test')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/admin' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Add Product').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Submit new product form with valid data and confirm success state
description: Verifies the add-product form accepts valid input and returns the user to the products list with the new item visible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin/login as the test explicitly requests (use navigate action to /admin/login on the same host).
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'admin' into the username field (index 1241), type 'password123' into the password field (index 1242), then click the Sign in button (index 1245).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Products' navigation item in the admin sidebar (use interactive element index 1508).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/aside/nav/a[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'إضافة منتج جديد' (Add Product) button to open the add-product form (use index 1619).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/main/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type 'Test Product A' into the Name field (index 2098) and then click the Save/Add button (index 2162). ASSERTION: After saving, the dialog should close and the products list should show the new product.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Product A')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/admin/products' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Test Product A').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Add-product dialog remained open after clicking the Save button, so the UI did not return the user to the products list.
- A validation error notification is displayed: 'بيانات ناقصة يرجى تعبئة الاسم والسعر والقسم وكود المنتج.' indicating required fields were missing.
- Required fields (code, price, and main category) were not filled when Save was clicked.
- Presence of 'Test Product A' in the products table cannot be attributed to a successful save because the dialog remained open and a validation error was shown.

title: TC004-Products list loads: verify products table is visible after opening Products page
description: Verifies the Products management page loads successfully and displays the products list UI.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin/login (use explicit navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login) to load the admin login page.
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'admin' into the username field (element index 1130).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Products' navigation link in the admin sidebar to open the Products management page (use interactive element index 1387).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/aside/nav/a[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div[2]/main/div/div/div[1]/button').nth(0)
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Expected 'إضافة منتج جديد' (Add Product) button to be visible on Products page"
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div[2]/main/div/div/div[3]/div[1]/div/table/tbody/tr/td/svg').nth(0)
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Expected products table to be visible on Products page (verified via svg inside table)"
        raise AssertionError("Cannot verify page title contains 'Products': no element with the text 'Products' found in the available elements; the page content appears to be Arabic (e.g., 'المنتجات').")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Add a new category successfully and see it in the categories table
description: Verifies an admin can create a category and that it appears in the categories list after saving.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /admin/login (use explicit navigate to /admin/login as required by test steps)
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'admin' into the username field (index 52), type 'password123' into the password field (index 56), then click the Log in button (index 59).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'الأقسام' (Categories) in the admin navigation to open the categories list (use interactive element index 273).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/aside/nav/a[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'إضافة قسم رئيسي' (Add Category) button to open the add-category form (use element index 384).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/main/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the category form fields (Arabic name, English name, slug) and click the save button to create the category. After saving, verify 'Seasonal Specials' is visible in the categories list. Note: the dialog does not include a Description field, will report that as missing after save if required.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('عروض موسمية')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Seasonal Specials')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('seasonal-specials-test')
        
        # -> Click the save button to submit the new category and then verify the category appears in the categories list.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert "/admin" in frame.url
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await elem.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=5000)
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        assert await elem.input_value() == 'Seasonal Specials'
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Category creation validation: name is required
description: Verifies the category form blocks saving when the name is blank and shows a validation message.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:5002
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Use the explicit test instruction to navigate to /admin/login (http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login) so the login form can be accessed.
        await page.goto("http://127.0.0.1:5002/admin/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the login form with username 'admin' (index 100) and password 'password123' (index 104), then click the login button (index 107).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('admin')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[4]/div[2]/form/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Categories' in the admin navigation to open the Categories page (use the visible 'الأقسام' link).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/aside/nav/a[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'إضافة قسم رئيسي' (Add main category) button to open the category creation form (interactive element index 428).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/main/div/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Save button on the Add Category modal (index 1027) with the name fields left blank, then check the page for the validation text 'Name is required' (and Arabic equivalent 'الاسم مطلوب') and confirm the category form/modal is still visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/admin' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Name is required').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//form[@id="category-form"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Validation message 'Name is required' (English) or 'الاسم مطلوب' (Arabic) not found on the page after clicking Save with empty name.
- The form accepted the Save action without displaying any validation error, indicating saving was not blocked client-side when name fields were blank.
- No inline error text was visible adjacent to the name input fields (ids: nameAr, nameEn) after the save attempt.

title: TC007-Edit an existing category successfully and see updates in the table
description: Verifies an admin can edit a category and the updated values appear in the categories table.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()
